pages.raw_text:
The members of the local drama
tic club, who held their first meet
ing last Thursday evening with
Miss Annette Oudin, will meet this
evening with Miss Edith Brown
less at her home on Eighth av-
enue. The club will present, "A
Night OFF, under the direction of
Miss Serruys.


The testimonial concert to be
given for Miss Jessie Rice will take
place this evening at the First
Methodist church. A number of
the best local artists, including
Miss Olivia Dahl, Miss Rice, Carl
Grissen, Arthur Frazer andy George
Greenwood, will take part and the
program promises TO be most en-
PERTAINING one.


The officers and members of the
Y. w. G. A. will hold a reception
this afternoon for their new secre-
tary, Miss Elsie Whiting, at the
parlors OF the association in the
Auditorium building. Invitations
have been issued to the 400 mem
bers of the society and the affair
will undoubtedly be one of the
largest events of the season among
the club members.


Mrs. e. p. Galbraith will leave
today for an extended trip to the
coast. She will probably be gone
all winter.


Mrs. VIII Miller of Reardan is
spending a week in the city as the
guest OF her sister, Mrs. A. w.
Brown, at her home on East
Spraeue avenue.


The domestic science depart
ment of the Woman's club held
their regular demonstration meet
ing this afternoon at the North
Central high school. Mrs. Lucy
b. Hall, instructor.


The regular board meeting was
held at 80 p. m. and the club
meeting at o'clock.


& 4 =


The Spokane lodge No. 2, De-
gree of Honor, will give a card
party this evening in lower Pacific
hall.
